summary: Harden rust-diffforge desktop auth startup so users cannot remain stuck on the launch screen and every auth network path is bounded.

review_items.summary: Desktop auth startup has a hard 5 second watchdog that falls back to signed-out web login.
Deep-link listener registration happens immediately and startup auth completion is idempotent without a StrictMode-breaking initializedRef guard.
Late results from timed-out startup, session validation, or code exchange are ignored after the auth flow id changes.
Session restore, auth code exchange, plan refresh, and logout all use bounded client timeouts.
Project policy now records the production desktop auth invariants for startup, deep links, server validation, billing gate, and logout.
The launch loader uses a blue base track with a one-way orange segment that enters, crosses, and exits.
The desktop launch screen automatically checks the Diff Forge API hello endpoint and only exposes signed-out login after backend reachability succeeds.
If the API hello check fails, the launch screen switches to a polished offline state, stops the moving loading indicator, and offers an explicit retry.
The desktop backend ping command returns stable generic online copy instead of echoing service-provided names into app UI.

rules: Startup auth must transition to authenticated, pricing-gated, or signed-out login state within 5 seconds.
Auth startup must not wait on deep-link listener registration after saved-session validation starts.
React StrictMode effect cleanup/setup cycles must not permanently skip auth initialization.
All desktop auth network commands must have client-side and Tauri-side bounds.
Late async results must be ignored after the active auth flow id changes.
Timeout fallback clears stale local auth data and keeps the web sign-in button available.
Dashboard rendering remains gated by server-returned user.planStatus paid.
The signed-out login screen must not render until the automatic backend hello check has succeeded.
The automatic backend hello check must have a bounded client-side timeout.
Backend hello failure must keep users on a launch/offline state with a stopped loader and retry action instead of a stale sign-in screen.

cross_project_impact.contract: No shape change to the existing /api/hello or /api/desktop/* contracts. rust-diffforge becomes stricter about local startup deadlines, backend reachability gating, and late result handling.
cross_project_impact.compatibility_notes: Existing deployed Next.js desktop auth endpoints remain compatible.
